ci_support_linux_64_.yaml:
c_compiler:
- gcc
c_compiler_version:
- '14'
c_stdlib:
- sysroot
c_stdlib_version:
- '2.17'
channel_sources:
- conda-forge
channel_targets:
- conda-forge main
docker_image:
- quay.io/condaforge/linux-anvil-x86_64:alma9
target_platform:
- linux-64

about.description:
aws-vault is a tool to securely store and access AWS credentials in a
development environment. It stores IAM credentials in your operating
system's secure keystore and then generates temporary credentials from
those to expose to your shell and applications. It's designed to be
complementary to the AWS CLI tools, and is aware of your profiles and
configuration in ~/.aws/config.

This is the ByteNess fork of the original 99designs/aws-vault project.
about.summary: A vault for securely storing and accessing AWS credentials in development environments.

script: go-licenses save . --save_path ../library_licenses${{ ' --ignore github.com/mattn/go-localereader' if win else '' }}

name: aws-vault

about.description:
aws-vault is a tool to securely store and access AWS credentials in a
development environment. It stores IAM credentials in your operating
system's secure keystore and then generates temporary credentials from
those to expose to your shell and applications. It's designed to be
complementary to the AWS CLI tools, and is aware of your profiles and
configuration in ~/.aws/config.

This is the ByteNess fork of the original 99designs/aws-vault project.
about.summary: A vault for securely storing and accessing AWS credentials in development environments.

script: go-licenses save . --save_path ../library_licenses${{ ' --ignore github.com/mattn/go-localereader' if win else '' }}

raw_meta_yaml:
context:
  version: "7.9.15"

package:
  name: aws-vault
  version: ${{ version }}

source:
  - url: https://github.com/ByteNess/aws-vault/archive/refs/tags/v${{ version }}.tar.gz
    sha256: e6b924a2b370021cb4e3b59f73dabb083fd5bb925b630cc211b47733bb5596b2
    target_directory: src
  - if: win
    then:
      - url: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mattn/go-localereader/refs/heads/master/LICENSE
        sha256: dceede03550621e65ec7b79fcba87cf6dbb9d31132a8ae71a8fe8ceec0af7da3
        file_name: LICENSE-go-localereader

build:
  number: 0
  script:
    - cd src
    - go-licenses save . --save_path ../library_licenses${{ ' --ignore github.com/mattn/go-localereader' if win else '' }}
    - if: osx
      then:
        # touchid-go's cgo adds -fmodules, which implicitly loads the SDK's
        # libxml modulemap and conflicts with conda-forge's libxml2. The
        # package uses #import and already links -framework LocalAuthentication,
        # so dropping the flag is safe.
        - go mod vendor
        - sed -i.bak 's/ -fmodules//g' vendor/github.com/noamcohen97/touchid-go/*.go
        - rm vendor/github.com/noamcohen97/touchid-go/*.bak
        - grep -rn fmodules vendor/github.com/noamcohen97/touchid-go/ && exit 1 || true
    - if: unix
      then:
        - go build -v -o $PREFIX/bin/aws-vault -ldflags="-s -w -X main.Version=v${{ version }}" -trimpath .
        - mkdir -p $PREFIX/share/bash-completion/completions $PREFIX/share/zsh/site-functions $PREFIX/share/fish/vendor_completions.d
        - cp contrib/completions/bash/aws-vault.bash $PREFIX/share/bash-completion/completions/aws-vault
        - cp contrib/completions/zsh/aws-vault.zsh $PREFIX/share/zsh/site-functions/_aws-vault
        - cp contrib/completions/fish/aws-vault.fish $PREFIX/share/fish/vendor_completions.d/aws-vault.fish
      else:
        - go build -v -o %LIBRARY_BIN%\aws-vault.exe -ldflags="-s -w -X main.Version=v${{ version }}" -trimpath .

requirements:
  build:
    - ${{ compiler("go-cgo") }}
    - ${{ compiler("c") }}
    - ${{ stdlib("c") }}
    - go-licenses

tests:
  - script:
      - aws-vault --help
      - aws-vault --version
      - if: unix
        then:
          - aws-vault --version 2>&1 | grep ${{ version }}
  - package_contents:
      bin:
        - aws-vault
      files:
        - if: unix
          then:
            - share/bash-completion/completions/aws-vault
            - share/zsh/site-functions/_aws-vault
            - share/fish/vendor_completions.d/aws-vault.fish
      strict: true

about:
  homepage: https://github.com/ByteNess/aws-vault
  summary: A vault for securely storing and accessing AWS credentials in development environments.
  description: |
    aws-vault is a tool to securely store and access AWS credentials in a
    development environment. It stores IAM credentials in your operating
    system's secure keystore and then generates temporary credentials from
    those to expose to your shell and applications. It's designed to be
    complementary to the AWS CLI tools, and is aware of your profiles and
    configuration in ~/.aws/config.

    This is the ByteNess fork of the original 99designs/aws-vault project.
  license: MIT
  license_file:
    - src/LICENSE
    - library_licenses/
    - if: win
      then:
        - LICENSE-go-localereader
  repository: https://github.com/ByteNess/aws-vault

extra:
  recipe-maintainers:
    - pavelzw
